21.correct: Mulk Raj Anand's novel 'Untouchable' centres on a day in the life of Bakha, a young sweeper, exposing caste discrimination.

wrong.A: 'The Guide' was written by R.K. Narayan.
wrong.C: 'Train to Pakistan' was written by Khushwant Singh.
wrong.D: 'The White Tiger' was written by Aravind Adiga.

21.fact: 'Untouchable' (1935) is one of the earliest Indian English novels of social protest.

22.correct: Anita Desai's 'Clear Light of Day' is set in Old Delhi, following a family grappling with memories and the aftermath of Partition.

wrong.A: It is not set in a Himalayan village.
wrong.B: It is not set in wartime Bombay.
wrong.D: It is not about an American academic in Mexico.

22.fact: 'Clear Light of Day' was shortlisted for the Booker Prize.

23.correct: The World Happiness Report aims to measure human well-being and guide policy towards greater human happiness.

wrong.A: It tracks more than just poverty.
wrong.B: It ranks all countries, not just developing ones.
wrong.C: It is not designed for investment decisions.

23.fact: It uses six factors including GDP per capita, social support and freedom of choice.

24.correct: A modern field-hockey match is played in four quarters of 15 minutes each (since 2014).

wrong.A: Two 35-minute halves was the older format.

24.fact: The four-quarter format gives more breaks for strategy and recovery.

25.correct: The 14th BRICS Summit was held in 2022 (hosted by China, in virtual format from Beijing).
25.fact: BRICS summits are held annually, with the chairship rotating among members.

26.correct: A deficiency of insulin in the body can cause diabetes (mellitus), as blood sugar is not regulated.

wrong.A: Goitre is caused by iodine deficiency.
wrong.C: Dwarfism is linked to growth-hormone deficiency.
wrong.D: Hypertension is high blood pressure, not caused by insulin deficiency.

26.fact: Insulin, secreted by the pancreas, lowers blood glucose levels.

27.correct: The Kalinga War is considered the turning point that led Ashoka to embrace Dhamma after witnessing its bloodshed.

wrong.A: His coronation was not the turning point.
wrong.C: A treaty with Sri Lanka was not the cause.
wrong.D: The Taxila revolt was not the turning point.

27.fact: After the Kalinga War, Ashoka renounced violent conquest in favour of 'conquest by Dhamma'.

28.correct: The Narasimham Committee (1991) banking reforms included all of these - capital adequacy norms, interest-rate deregulation and reduction of non-performing assets.

wrong.A: Capital adequacy norms were one recommendation, not the only one.
wrong.B: Interest-rate deregulation was one of several.
wrong.C: NPA reduction was one of several.

28.fact: The Narasimham Committee reports (1991 and 1998) shaped India's banking-sector reforms.

29.correct: In Chola administration, the 'Ur' was the assembly responsible for village-level governance (in ordinary villages).

wrong.A: It was not for military recruitment.
wrong.B: It was not for temple construction.
wrong.D: It was not for collecting taxes from foreign traders.

29.fact: The Cholas had a sophisticated local self-government system, including the Ur, Sabha and Nagaram.

30.correct: 'Stagflation' is the situation of high inflation combined with high unemployment (and stagnant growth).

wrong.A: It is not inflation with a trade surplus.
wrong.B: It is not inflation with rising employment.
wrong.C: It is not inflation with rising GDP.

30.fact: Stagflation is hard to tackle because fixing inflation and unemployment require opposite policies.

31.correct: The 'Loo' is a hot, dry local wind that blows over the northern plains of India in summer.

wrong.B: It is not cold and dry.
wrong.C: It is not hot and humid.
wrong.D: It is not cold and moist.

31.fact: The Loo can cause heatstroke; it blows during the day in May and June.

32.correct: Only statement II is correct - Article 40 deals with the organisation of village panchayats. Statement I is wrong because securing a social order for welfare is Article 38 (not Article 39). So 'Only II'.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong; statement I misattributes the 'social order' principle to Article 39.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since statement I is incorrect.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since statement II (Article 40) is correct.

32.fact: Article 38 secures a social order for welfare; Article 39 lists specific policy principles; Article 40 provides for village panchayats.

33.correct: India's next Census is to be conducted in 2 phases (house-listing and population enumeration).

wrong.B: It is not done in 3 phases.

33.fact: The upcoming Census will also include a caste enumeration and will be India's first digital census.

34.correct: On 9 May 2025 the Supreme Court observed that it is not the courts' duty to direct media to take down or remove content.

wrong.A: It was not the Allahabad High Court.
wrong.B: It was not the Law Commission.
wrong.D: It was not the Patna High Court.

34.fact: The observation related to freedom of the press and judicial restraint.

35.correct: Neither pair is correct - 'Adoption and succession' is on the Concurrent List (not the State List), and 'Trade and commerce' (within a state) is largely a State List subject (inter-state trade is Union). So 'Neither I nor II'.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong; adoption/succession is Concurrent.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since both pairings are incorrect.

35.fact: The division of subjects is given in the Seventh Schedule (Union, State and Concurrent Lists).

36.correct: In Maharashtra, 'Gudi Padwa' marks the start of the new year.

wrong.A: Baisakhi is the Punjabi new year/harvest festival.
wrong.B: Ugadi is the new year in Karnataka/Andhra/Telangana.
wrong.C: Onam is a Kerala harvest festival.

36.fact: On Gudi Padwa, a decorated 'gudi' (flag/pole) is displayed outside homes.

37.correct: ISRO's Small Satellite Launch Vehicle (SSLV) has a lift-off mass of about 120 tonnes.

wrong.A: 420 tonnes is far too heavy for the SSLV.

37.fact: The SSLV is designed for quick, low-cost launches of small satellites to low Earth orbit.

38.correct: The soles of shoes have grooves (treads) to provide better grip and safety by increasing friction.

wrong.A: It is not to make them softer.
wrong.B: It is not mainly for design.
wrong.D: It is not to reduce weight.

38.fact: Treads increase friction with the ground, preventing slipping.

39.correct: Thermal energy (from fossil fuels) is NOT a non-conventional energy resource - it is a conventional source.
39.fact: Non-conventional (renewable) sources include solar, wind, tidal, geothermal and biomass.

40.correct: The first IPL final in 2008 was played at the DY Patil Stadium in Navi Mumbai.
40.fact: Rajasthan Royals won the inaugural 2008 IPL title there.
